verdicts.note: Evidence confirms an MCP server exists at GitHub repos iPAYX-Technologies/mcp-fx-audit and Ipayx-stellar/mcp-fx-audit, plus third-party registry listing
GitHub URL https://github.com/iPAYX-Technologies/mcp-fx-audit is valid and confirmed in evidence
Evidence doesn't show ipayx.ai/docs website - only references to ipayx.ai in GitHub commits and documentation.ixopay.com URLs
FX audit and payment processing falls under Finance and Fintech
Evidence confirms MCP server for FX audit (free, mostly no-signup) and separate IXOPAY payment platform APIs with various auth methods
Evidence confirms API key for MCP (optional Bearer token), Basic Auth for IXOPAY Transaction/Provisioning/Settlement APIs, and HMAC signature for Gateway API
MCP FX audit tools work with no signup per README, free API key obtainable per description

Evidence shows IXOPAY as a separate payment platform (ixopay.com) requiring plan upgrades for full features, not part of iPayX. Provisioning process for IXOPAY involves creating API keys through their dashboard, not typical PSP partnership model
REST APIs confirmed for IXOPAY platform; MCP server exposed via HTTP endpoint
MCP has 4 tools, IXOPAY has multiple APIs - medium breadth is reasonable
MCP server is accessible via public endpoint, IXOPAY APIs are accessible with proper credentials
No blocker for MCP server usage
Should be higher given clear evidence of MCP server existence and functionality

IXOPAY is clearly a separate company/platform (ixopay.com, IXOPAY GmbH) not affiliated with iPayX. Evidence shows IXOPAY requires upgrades for full API access, contradicting 'partnership' characterization

revised.name: iPayX
revised.one_liner: iPayX provides an FX forensic-audit MCP server (free, mostly no-signup). IXOPAY is a separate payment platform with Transaction/Provisioning/Settlement/Gateway APIs using Basic Auth and HMAC signing.

auth_methods.notes: MCP FX Audit tool: Bearer token via IPAYX_API_KEY env var, optional, required only for live provider quotes/full reports.
IXOPAY Transaction/Provisioning/Settlement APIs use HTTP Basic Auth (Base64 of username:password).
IXOPAY Gateway API requires HMAC-SHA hash signature in Authorization header.

revised.self_serve_notes: Core FX audit MCP tools (mid-market rate lookups) work with no signup/API key. Free API key obtainable via iPayX website for live provider comparisons and forensic reports; some advanced reporting is billed per report. IXOPAY is a separate platform (ixopay.com) requiring paid plan upgrades for full API access.

api_surface.notes: MCP server exposes 4 tools (audit_fx_fee, compare_providers, score_invoice, get_mid_market_rate / check_fx_rate). IXOPAY is a separate company's platform with its own REST APIs.

evidence.supports: MCP server exists, free/no-signup basic usage, tools list, live endpoint URL
MCP server package (@ipayx/mcp-fx-audit) exposing 4 FX audit tools
Third-party MCP registry listing confirming existence of iPayX MCP server
IXOPAY Transaction API uses Basic Auth
IXOPAY Provisioning API uses Basic Auth
IXOPAY Settlement API uses Basic Auth
API key/username setup for Basic Auth credentials
Gateway API requires HMAC signature in Authorization header

flags: IXOPAY (ixopay.com) is a separate payment platform company, not affiliated with iPayX
MCP server is a lightweight FX rate comparison tool, not a full payments API
Live endpoint URL differs between README (wild-bird-a412.ybolduc.workers.dev) and stated official endpoint (mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp), suggesting in-progress migration
